behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_GARGOYLE::look' (examine) event for a gargoyle object. When triggered it associates an info/description entry with the target object and makes the object emit a short label string "gargoyle ".

Sequence: set an info id (0x208) on the object, adjust process state (process exclude), construct the literal string "gargoyle ", call Item::bark(object, "gargoyle ") to display/emit that label, free the temporary string, propagate the bark return value, then suspend.
This is a presentation/description routine: it does not change persistent global quest flags and primarily provides UI feedback when the player/actor inspects the gargoyle.
notable_control_flow: Entry (Event 0 'U8P_GARGOYLE::look') -> push info id 0x208 and object -> set info on object -> process exclude (adjusts call context) -> build literal string "gargoyle " -> Item::bark(object, string) -> free string -> return bark retval -> suspend.
Control flow is linear and short-circuited: no branching or global-state mutation is observed; the routine's visible effect is the bark and the setting of the info id on the object.
The routine issues a suspend after preparing the return value, indicating it executes in a process context (execution_form: process) and yields control back to the engine afterward.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of info id 0x208 (what description or state it represents) — inferred to be a U8P_GARGOYLE::look/description id but not proven.
Precise effect of 'process exclude' in this context (what processing is being excluded and for how long) — inferred to alter process flags before the bark call.
Who is the effective recipient of Item::bark in multi-process contexts (the object itself acting as a process vs. the player's process) — inferred but not explicit in disassembly.
Whether the bark return value is used by the caller or solely returned for engine bookkeeping (inferred to be propagated but downstream usage is unknown).

main_function.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the in-game description/label for a gargoyle when it is examined: mark the object with an info id and emit the short descriptive string "gargoyle ".
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GARGOYLE::look) — when the player/avatar looks at or examines the gargoyle (area/interaction 'U8P_GARGOYLE::look' event).

function_map.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::look
function_map.role: Handles the U8P_GARGOYLE::look/examine event for the gargoyle object and drives description display.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine invoked to display or emit a short text string from an item/process.

function_map.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::set info
function_map.role: Engine operation to associate an info/description id (0x208) with the target object.

function_map.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine/process-state opcode that alters process handling for the ensuing operation (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::free string
function_map.role: Deallocate the temporary string buffer used for the bark call.

external_calls.purpose: Associate info id 0x208 with the target object so that the U8P_GARGOYLE::look event provides that info/description.
Adjust process state/flags before calling Item::bark (likely to prevent re-entrancy or additional processing during bark).
Cause the object to emit/display the literal label "gargoyle " to the relevant viewer/process.
Release the temporary string buffer allocated to hold "gargoyle ".

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_GARGOYLE::look' (examine) event for a gargoyle object. When triggered it associates an info/description entry with the target object and makes the object emit a short label string "gargoyle ".
